item.text: I’ll treat this as a read-only, independent audit. I’ll first load the project’s Beads workflow context, then inspect the frozen registry, amendments, generator, log chain, analysis, and provenance before reproducing the verdict.

item.aggregated_output:
[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""verdict-gen — the verdict as a pure function of (frozen record, chained log).
     3	
     4	    python3 tools/registry/verdict-gen.py --experiment <id> --agent-id coordinator-1
     5	        [--root <repo>] [--computed-at 2026-07-12T18:00:00Z]
     6	
     7	Implements P2 §3.1 (minimal spine — the F1-critical path):
     8	  1. Load registry/experiments/<id>.json; recompute the frozen hash; require
     9	     equality with registry/frozen-index.json (ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT — no verdict
    10	     of any kind is producible from a drifted record).
    11	  2. Amendment overlay (P2 §1.4 / §3.1 step 2): apply kot-amend/1 records from
    12	     registry/amendments/<id>/ in seq order as an overlay on the frozen record
    13	     BEFORE readout. kind "ops" / "pre-authorized-fallback" amendments may ONLY
    14	     fill a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder or add a new runtime pin under /pins/;
    15	     a patch touching design scope (endpoints, thresholds, verdict_rules, kill
    16	     text, baselines, scale rungs, the pinned analysis script) aborts with
    17	     ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND. kind "design" amendments are REFUSED once the RT-5
    18	     cutoff has passed — the experiment is GNG-0-signed or its log contains a
    19	     phase:"final" run record — with ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT (the only lawful path
    20	     is a new experiment id with `supersedes`). An invalid amendment always
    21	     aborts; it is never silently skipped. The frozen record file and its
    22	     frozen_sha256 anchor are untouched; the deterministic overlay result is
    23	     logged in the verdict object as inputs.amended_record_sha256 alongside
    24	     inputs.amendments_applied, so the amended record is independently
    25	     re-derivable and verifiable from (frozen record, amendment files).
    26	  3. Select eligible run records from results-log/<id>.jsonl (chain-verified):
    27	     event=="run", phase=="final", exit=="ok", not superseded,
    28	     prereg_hash == frozen_sha256, config.seed in design.seeds (when seeds are
    29	     registered), config values within declared IV level sets. Exclusions are
    30	     listed with the failed test named — never silent.
    31	  3b. D9 reused-row eligibility (resource-optimization-plan.md §3 revision-1):
    32	     rows of ANOTHER record's log enter the analysis only via a frozen
    33	     kot-reg/2 reused_from block that re-verifies at consumption time
    34	     (RC-1..RC-8, maintainer-ratified ruling, filled pins, producer chain +
    35	     row hashes intact) AND an in-chain event:"reuse" witness restating the
    36	     exact row set. A reuse witness without a frozen declaration refuses
    37	     (ERR_P2_REUSE_UNDECLARED); >=1 fresh eligible run row is still required.
    38	  4. Completeness gate (minimal): an empty eligible set => INCOMPLETE-DATA.
    39	  5. Run the pinned analysis script (sha256 re-verified at execution;
    40	     ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT on mismatch) with the eligible records as JSONL on
    41	     stdin; its stdout JSON is written to reports/auto/<id>/analysis-output.json.
    42	     ALL derived statistics live there and nowhere else (G-4).
    43	  6. Write the `unblind` log line (first time only) via log-append — the single
    44	     write path.
    45	  7. Evaluate the frozen verdict_rules top-down with the minimal expression
    46	     grammar (kot_common.eval_expr). A missing/null metric pointer => verdict
    47	     INCOMPLETE-DATA (fail closed — an analysis bug cannot default to a verdict).
    48	  8. A fired PASS is emitted as PASS-PENDING-AUDIT unless a CONFIRMED audit
    49	     record by a non-runner identity exists under registry/audits/<id>/ (G-6).
    50	  9. Emit registry/verdicts/<id>.json (canonical JSON; RT-14-linted).
    51	
    52	The rendered markdown report (P2 §3.3) is S7 report-gen's job — not built here.
    53	"""
    54

    68	# P2 §1.4 design scope — JSON-pointer prefixes that an "ops" /
    69	# "pre-authorized-fallback" amendment may NEVER touch (endpoints and
    70	# verdict_rules carry the thresholds; design carries baselines, scale rungs,
    71	# seeds, IV levels; the kill/envelope text and the pinned analysis script are
    72	# the frozen contract). Touching any of these is a design change and fails
    73	# closed with ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND.
    74	DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES = (
    75	    "/endpoints",
    76	    "/verdict_rules",
    77	    "/design",
    78	    "/kill_criterion_verbatim",
    79	    "/extrapolation_envelope_verbatim",
    80	    "/pins/analysis_script",
    81	    "/hypotheses",
    82	    "/analysis_plan_ref",
    83	    "/prereg_doc",
    84	    "/coverage_requirement",
    85	    "/efficiency_relevant",
    86	    # D9: the reuse declaration is design scope — which rows a record consumes,
    87	    # under which RC basis, may never move post-freeze by ops amendment.
    88	    "/reused_from",
    89	    "/reuse_overrides",
    90	)
    91	
    92	# kot-reg schema selection (D9: kot-reg/2 records carry the reuse surface).
    93	SCHEMA_FILES = {"kot-reg/1": "kot-reg-1.json", "kot-reg/2": "kot-reg-2.json"}
    94	
    95	# Freeze bookkeeping — no amendment of ANY kind may touch these.
    96	FREEZE_FIELD_PREFIXES = ("/status", "/frozen_sha256", "/frozen_at", "/frozen_by", "/id", "/schema_version")
    97	
    98	# An ops pin value is a bare hex digest: 40 hex (git/HF revision sha) or
    99	# 64 hex (sha256: corpus digest, staged-bytes manifest). Nothing else.
   100	PIN_VALUE_RE = re.compile(r"^([0-9a-f]{40}|[0-9a-f]{64})$")
   101	
   102	
   103	def _touches(path, prefixes):
   104	    return any(path == p or path.startswith(p + "/") for p in prefixes)
   105	
   106	
   107	def fail(code, msg):
   108	    print("%s: %s" % (code, msg), file=sys.stderr)
   109	    sys.exit(1)

   112	def file_sha256(path):
   113	    h = hashlib.sha256()
   114	    with open(path, "rb") as f:
   115	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(1 << 20), b""):
   116	            h.update(chunk)
   117	    return h.hexdigest()

   120	def gng0_signed(root, exp_id):
   121	    """True iff the experiment's frozen record is covered by the GNG-0 signoff."""
   122	    path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "gng0-signoff.json")
   123	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
   124	        return False
   125	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   126	        signoff = json.load(f)
   127	    return exp_id in signoff.get("frozen_records", {})

   129	
   130	def load_amendments(root, exp_id):
   131	    """Load, schema-validate, and RT-14-lint registry/amendments/<id>/*.json.
   132	
   133	    Returns [(relpath, amendment)] sorted by seq. Any malformed record aborts
   134	    (ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT) — an unreadable amendment is never skipped.
   135	    """
   136	    paths = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "amendments", exp_id, "*.json")))
   137	    if not paths:
   138	        return []
   139	    schema_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "schema", "kot-amend-1.json")
   140	    with open(schema_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   141	        schema = json.load(f)
   142	    out, seen = [], set()
   143	    for path in paths:
   144	        rel = os.path.relpath(path, root)
   145	        try:
   146	            with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   147	                am = json.load(f)
   148	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
   149	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: unparseable (%s)" % (rel, e))
   150	        errs = kc.validate_schema(am, schema)
   151	        if errs:
   152	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: %s" % (rel, "; ".join(errs[:5])))
   153	        if am["experiment"] != exp_id:
   154	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   155	                              "%s: experiment %r != %r" % (rel, am["experiment"], exp_id))
   156	        prefix = os.path.basename(path).split("-", 1)[0]
   157	        if not prefix.isdigit() or int(prefix) != am["seq"]:
   158	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   159	                              "%s: filename seq prefix does not match seq=%r (<seq>-<slug>.json)"
   160	                              % (rel, am["seq"]))
   161	        if am["seq"] in seen:
   162	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT", "%s: duplicate amendment seq %d" % (rel, am["seq"]))
   163	        seen.add(am["seq"])
   164	        kc.check_identity_fields(am)
   165	        kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(am), rel)
   166	        out.append((rel, am))
   167	    out.sort(key=lambda t: t[1]["seq"])
   168	    return out
   169	
   170	
   171	def apply_amendment_overlay(root, exp_id, record, log_records):
   172	    """P2 §3.1 step 2: apply valid amendments in seq order as an overlay.
   173	
   174	    Returns (effective_record, applied_seqs, effective_sha256-or-None). The
   175	    frozen record file is never touched; the overlay is a pure function of
   176	    (frozen record, amendment files) so effective_sha256 is re-derivable by
   177	    any verifier. Fails closed on the first invalid amendment.
   178	    """
   179	    amendments = load_amendments(root, exp_id)
   180	    if not amendments:
   181	        return record, [], None
   182	
   183	    signed = gng0_signed(root, exp_id)
   184	    has_final = any(r.get("event") == "run" and r.get("phase") == "final" for r in log_records)
   185	    effective = record
   186	    applied = []
   187	    for rel, am in amendments:
   188	        for j, op in enumerate(am["patch"]):
   189	            if _touches(op["path"], FREEZE_FIELD_PREFIXES):
   190	                raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   191	                                  "%s patch[%d]: freeze bookkeeping field %s may never be amended"
   192	                                  % (rel, j, op["path"]))
   193	        if am["kind"] == "design":
   194	            # RT-5 cutoff: design amendments die at first raw-data exposure
   195	            # (first phase:"final" run record) — and a GNG-0-signed record's
   196	            # design is immutable outright. The only lawful path afterwards is
   197	            # a new experiment id with `supersedes` and a fresh freeze.
   198	            if signed or has_final:
   199	                raise kc.KotError(
   200	                    "ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   201	                    "%s: design amendment after the RT-5 cutoff (GNG-0-signed=%s, final-phase run "
   202	                    "present=%s) — the frozen design is immutable; supersede with a new experiment id"
   203	                    % (rel, signed, has_final))
   204	        else:  # "ops" | "pre-authorized-fallback"
   205	            if am["kind"] == "pre-authorized-fallback":
   206	                ptr = am.get("pre_authorized_by")
   207	                if not ptr:
   208	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   209	                                      "%s: pre-authorized-fallback must name the frozen-record field "
   210	                                      "that pre-authorized it (pre_authorized_by)" % rel)
   211	                if kc.resolve_pointer(record, ptr) is kc._MISSING:
   212	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   213	                                      "%s: pre_authorized_by pointer %r is not present in the frozen record"
   214	                                      % (rel, ptr))
   215	            for j, op in enumerate(am["patch"]):
   216	                where = "%s patch[%d] (%s %s)" % (rel, j, op["op"], op["path"])
   217	                if _touches(op["path"], DESIGN_SCOPE_PREFIXES):
   218	                    raise kc.KotError(
   219	                        "ERR_P2_DESIGN_AMEND",
   220	                        "%s: ops-scope amendment touches design scope (endpoints / thresholds / "
   221	                        "verdict_rules / kill text / baselines / scale rungs / pinned analysis) — "
   222	                        "refused; a design change needs a new experiment id" % where)
   223	                if not op["path"].startswith("/pins/"):
   224	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   225	                                      "%s: an ops amendment may only fill a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder "
   226	                                      "or add a runtime pin under /pins/" % where)
   227	                if op["op"] == "remove":
   228	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   229	                                      "%s: ops amendments may not remove pins" % where)
   230	                value = op.get("value")
   231	                if not isinstance(value, str) or not PIN_VALUE_RE.match(value):
   232	                    raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   233	                                      "%s: pin value must be a bare 40- or 64-char lowercase hex digest, "
   234	                                      "got %r" % (where, value))
   235	                current = kc.resolve_pointer(effective, op["path"])
   236	                if op["op"] == "replace":
   237	                    if not (isinstance(current, str) and current.startswith(kc.PINNED_AT_INPUTS_PREFIX)):
   238	                        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   239	                                          "%s: replace target is not a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder "
   240	                                          "(current=%r) — frozen pins are immutable" % (where, current))
   241	                else:  # add
   242	                    if current is not kc._MISSING:
   243	                        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   244	                                          "%s: add target already exists — an existing pin cannot be "
   245	                                          "overwritten" % where)
   246	        effective = kc.json_patch_apply(effective, am["patch"])
   247	        applied.append(am["seq"])
   248	
   249	    # Post-overlay integrity: the effective record must still validate against
   250	    # ITS OWN schema version (kot-reg/1 or /2) and be RT-14-clean — an overlay
   251	    # cannot smuggle in what a freeze would have refused.
   252	    sv = effective.get("schema_version")
   253	    if sv not in SCHEMA_FILES:
   254	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   255	                          "amended effective record schema_version %r is not one of %s"
   256	                          % (sv, sorted(SCHEMA_FILES)))
   257	    schema_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "schema", SCHEMA_FILES[sv])
   258	    with open(schema_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   259	        reg_schema = json.load(f)
   260	    errs = kc.validate_schema(effective, reg_schema)
   261	    if errs:
   262	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_BAD_AMENDMENT",
   263	                          "amended effective record no longer validates against %s: %s"
   264	                          % (sv, "; ".join(errs[:5])))
   265	    kc.check_identity_fields(effective)
   266	    hashed = {k: v for k, v in effective.items() if k not in ("status", "frozen_sha256")}
   267	    kc.require_no_account_strings(kc.canonical_bytes(hashed), "amended effective record")
   268	    return effective, applied, kc.frozen_hash(effective)
   269	
   270	
   271	# Scale-language license total order for the min-rule (G-12 vocabulary is the
   272	# established one across all 17 issued verdicts: none / sign-only / slope).
   273	SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER = {"none": 0, "sign-only": 1, "slope": 2}
   274	
   275	
   276	def scale_language_license(design, rungs, analysis):
   277	    """Compute scale_language_licensed as the MINIMUM of three per-record caps.
   278	
   279	    The G-12 count map (docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664) is
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   280	    UNCHANGED: 1 rung -> "none", 2 -> "sign-only", >=3 -> "slope"-ELIGIBLE.
   281	    What changed (a5-llm Gate-A re-audit REFUTE 2026-07-11, adjudicated in
   282	    docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md section 2.1/2.2, maintainer-approved
   283	    2026-07-11 issue #5; correction records
   284	    registry/corrections/a5-llm/2-scale-license-erratum.json and
   285	    registry/corrections/f2/2-scale-license-erratum.json; residual defect fixed
   286	    per the cross-vendor re-audit
   287	    poc/gpt56-review/a5llm-reaudit-20260711/last-message.json point 3): the
   288	    licensed value is min over the license total order of
   289	
   290	      (i)   the G-12 TIER OF THE COMPARABLE-RUNG COUNT — when the frozen record
   291	            declares design.model_scale_rungs (the list of COMPARABLE
   292	            MODEL-SCALE rung labels, 08-stats-and-extrapolation.md section 2.1
   293	            precondition), the tier is computed over the count of MEASURED
   294	            rungs inside that list, NOT over raw measured labels. A baseline
   295	            label (a5-llm's R0, or any BASE* cell) can therefore inflate
   296	            NEITHER "sign-only"->"slope" NOR "none"->"sign-only" (the
   297	            2026-07-11 re-audit's point-3 residual: {measured=BASE0,BASE1,R1;
   298	            model_scale_rungs=[R1]} must license "none", one comparable rung).
   299	            A LEGACY record that never froze the field falls back to the raw
   300	            measured-label count — byte-identical to the pre-fix generator for
   301	            every issued license-"none"/"sign-only" verdict (f2b-replicate et
   302	            al.) — and "slope" stays unreachable there via caps (ii)+(iii).
   303	      (ii)  design.scale_language_max — the machine-readable frozen PER-RECORD
   304	            ceiling (the prose ceiling lives in extrapolation_envelope_verbatim;
   305	            this field is its machine-readable form, validated against that
   306	            text at freeze time by prereg-freeze). Absent field => no ceiling
   307	            permits slope => cap "sign-only" (fail closed for slope; the
   308	            legacy "none"/"sign-only" tiers pass through unchanged). A declared
   309	            ceiling below the tier also caps it (a declared "none" caps a
   310	            2-rung "sign-only"). A declared value OUTSIDE the license
   311	            vocabulary is unintelligible and caps at "none" (fail fully
   312	            closed; the schemas + prereg-freeze refuse it at freeze time).
   313	      (iii) the trend-validity gate — "slope" requires every JSON pointer in
   314	            design.scale_trend_valid_metrics to resolve to boolean true in the
   315	            analysis output: the registered section-2 trend machinery actually
   316	            RAN and produced a VALID result (for the a5-llm shape:
   317	            /gates/separation_valid plus the trend Holm member; a trend member
   318	            that left the family instrument-invalid can never license a
   319	            slope). Absent/empty metrics, a missing pointer, or no analysis at
   320	            all (INCOMPLETE-DATA path) => cap "sign-only".
   321	
   322	    Explicitly NOT a blunt ">=4 rungs" rule — that would rewrite G-12 /
   323	    08-stats section 2.1, which license a scale-trend fit at >=3 COMPARABLE
   324	    rungs, and that stays licensable here (3 comparable rungs + slope ceiling
   325	    + valid trend result => "slope").
   326	    """
   327	    model_rungs = design.get("model_scale_rungs")
   328	    if model_rungs is None:
   329	        # Legacy record: the field was never frozen. Raw measured-label count
   330	        # (pre-fix basis) keeps every issued 0/1/2-rung verdict byte-identical;
   331	        # "slope" is unreachable because caps (ii)+(iii) are also absent.
   332	        n_comparable = len(rungs)
   333	    else:
   334	        # Re-audit point 3: the tier is over the COUNT OF COMPARABLE rungs.
   335	        # A declared-empty list counts zero comparable rungs (=> "none") —
   336	        # the conservative reading, fail closed.
   337	        n_comparable = len([r for r in rungs if r in model_rungs])
   338	    tier = "none" if n_comparable < 2 else ("sign-only" if n_comparable == 2 else "slope")
   339	
   340	    ceiling = design.get("scale_language_max")
   341	    if ceiling is None:
   342	        ceiling_cap = "sign-only"  # absent ceiling: slope fails closed; lower tiers unaffected
   343	    elif ceiling in SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER:
   344	        ceiling_cap = ceiling
   345	    else:
   346	        ceiling_cap = "none"  # unintelligible declared ceiling: fail fully closed
   347	
   348	    trend_ptrs = design.get("scale_trend_valid_metrics") or []
   349	    trend_valid = (bool(trend_ptrs) and analysis is not None
   350	                   and all(kc.resolve_pointer(analysis, p) is True for p in trend_ptrs))
   351	    trend_cap = "slope" if trend_valid else "sign-only"
   352	
   353	    return min((tier, ceiling_cap, trend_cap), key=SCALE_LICENSE_ORDER.__getitem__)
   354	
   355	
   356	def select_eligible(records, frozen_sha, design):
   357	    """P2 §3.1 step 3. Returns (eligible, excluded=[{seq, reason}])."""
   358	    superseded = {r.get("target_seq") for r in records if r.get("event") == "supersede"}
   359	    iv_levels = {iv["name"]: iv["levels"] for iv in design.get("independent_vars", [])}
   360	    seeds = design.get("seeds", [])
   361	    eligible, excluded = [], []
   362	
   363	    def exclude(rec, reason):
   364	        excluded.append({"seq": rec["seq"], "reason": reason})
   365	
   366	    for rec in records:
   367	        if rec.get("event") != "run":
   368	            continue
   369	        if rec.get("phase") != "final":
   370	            exclude(rec, "phase!=final (%r)" % rec.get("phase"))
   371	            continue
   372	        if rec.get("exit") != "ok":
   373	            exclude(rec, "exit!=ok (%r)" % rec.get("exit"))
   374	            continue
   375	        if rec["seq"] in superseded:
   376	            exclude(rec, "superseded")
   377	            continue
   378	        if rec.get("prereg_hash") != frozen_sha:
   379	            exclude(rec, "prereg_hash mismatch")
   380	            continue
   381	        cfg = rec.get("config", {})
   382	        if seeds and "seed" in cfg and cfg["seed"] not in seeds:
   383	            exclude(rec, "seed %r not registered" % cfg["seed"])
   384	            continue
   385	        bad_level = None
   386	        for name, levels in iv_levels.items():
   387	            if name in cfg and cfg[name] not in levels:
   388	                bad_level = "config.%s=%r not in declared levels" % (name, cfg[name])
   389	                break
   390	        if bad_level:
   391	            exclude(rec, bad_level)
   392	            continue
   393	        eligible.append(rec)
   394	    return eligible, excluded
   395	
   396	
   397	def confirmed_audit(root, exp_id, runner_ids):
   398	    """Return the path of a CONFIRMED audit by a non-runner identity, or None."""
   399	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(root, "registry", "audits", exp_id, "*.json"))):
   400	        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   401	            audit = json.load(f)
   402	        if audit.get("outcome") != "CONFIRMED":
   403	            continue
   404	        auditor = audit.get("auditor")
   405	        if auditor in runner_ids:
   406	            raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_SELF_AUDIT",
   407	                              "%s: auditor %r also appears as a runner in the eligible log" % (path, auditor))
   408	        return os.path.relpath(path, root)
   409	    return None
   410	
   411	
   412	def main():
   413	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Compute an experiment verdict purely from frozen record + log.")
   414	    ap.add_argument("--experiment", required=True)
   415	    ap.add_argument("--agent-id", required=True, help="pseudonym stamped on the unblind log line")
   416	    ap.add_argument("--root", default=None)
   417	    ap.add_argument("--computed-at", default=None, help="UTC timestamp override (byte-determinism/tests)")
   418	    args = ap.parse_args()
   419	
   420	    root = args.root or os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
   421	    exp_id = args.experiment
   422	    try:
   423	        run(root, exp_id, args.agent_id, args.computed_at)
   424	    except kc.KotError as e:
   425	        fail(e.code, str(e).split(": ", 1)[1])
   426	    except FileNotFoundError as e:
   427	        fail("ERR_P2_IO", str(e))
   428	
   429	
   430	def run(root, exp_id, agent_id, computed_at):
   431	    kc.require_pseudonym(agent_id, "--agent-id")
   432	
   433	    # Step 1 — frozen record integrity.
   434	    rec_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "experiments", "%s.json" % exp_id)
   435	    index_path = os.path.join(root, "registry", "frozen-index.json")
   436	    with open(rec_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   437	        record = json.load(f)
   438	    with open(index_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   439	        index = json.load(f)
   440	    if exp_id not in index:
   441	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_NOT_FROZEN", "%s absent from frozen-index.json" % exp_id)
   442	    frozen_sha = index[exp_id]
   443	    if kc.frozen_hash(record) != frozen_sha or record.get("frozen_sha256") != frozen_sha:
   444	        raise kc.KotError("ERR_P2_FROZEN_DRIFT",
   445	                          "%s: frozen record bytes drifted from frozen-index — no verdict is producible" % exp_id)
   446	
   447	    # Step 2 — amendment overlay (fail closed, never silently skipped). The
   448	    # chain-verified log is read first: it is the RT-5 cutoff witness. The
   449	    # overlay never touches the frozen file; `record` stays the frozen anchor

   617	def is_real_pin(value):
   618	    """A pin value that PROVES identity: bare 40/64-hex, or <name>@<40/64-hex>.
   619	    Placeholders (PINNED-AT-INPUTS:*, POST-F2-INFRA-OPEN, anything else) are
   620	    indeterminate — fail-closed for identity comparisons."""
   621	    if not isinstance(value, str):
   622	        return False
   623	    return bool(SHA256_RE.match(value) or _HEX40_RE.match(value) or _REV_PIN_RE.search(value))

   626	def derive_artifact_rows(root, include_all=False):
   627	    """The artifact inventory as a PURE FUNCTION of results-log/*.jsonl +
   628	    registry/verdicts/ — derived LIVE (never from the committed ledger file, so
   629	    staleness cannot weaken a gate). One row per logged run row (final-phase
   630	    unless include_all), carrying the identity axes RC-2 compares: pins, seed,
   631	    config_sha256, impl pins (config keys ending _sha256), and row_sha256 (the
   632	    exact line bytes incl. newline — the RC-1 row-hash pin a consumer freezes).
   633	    Chain integrity is enforced by read_log (fail closed)."""
   634	    import glob as _glob
   635	    results_dir = os.path.join(root, "results-log")
   636	    verdicts_dir = os.path.join(root, "registry", "verdicts")
   637	    if not os.path.isdir(results_dir):
   638	        return []  # nothing logged yet — an empty inventory, not an error
   639	    verdicts = {
   640	        os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(p))[0]
   641	        for p in _glob.glob(os.path.join(verdicts_dir, "*.json"))
   642	    }
   643	    rows = []
   644	    for path in sorted(_glob.glob(os.path.join(results_dir, "*.jsonl"))):
   645	        exp = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(path))[0]
   646	        records, raw_lines = read_log(path)
   647	        for rec, raw in zip(records, raw_lines):
   648	            if rec.get("event") != "run" and not include_all:
   649	                continue
   650	            phase = rec.get("phase")
   651	            if phase != "final" and not include_all:
   652	                continue
   653	            metrics = rec.get("metrics") or {}
   654	            config = rec.get("config") or {}
   655	            per_item = sorted(k for k, v in metrics.items() if isinstance(v, list))
   656	            n_items = None
   657	            for k in ("item_correct", "record_caught"):
   658	                if isinstance(metrics.get(k), list):
   659	                    n_items = len(metrics[k])
   660	                    break
   661	            pins = rec.get("pins_observed") or {}
   662	            rows.append({
   663	                "schema_version": "kot-artifact/2",
   664	                "experiment": exp,
   665	                "log_path": os.path.relpath(path, root),
   666	                "seq": rec.get("seq"),
   667	                "phase": phase,
   668	                "event": rec.get("event"),

   670	                "config": config,

   672	                "impl_pins": {k: v for k, v in config.items()

   674	                "seed": config.get("seed"),
   675	                "n_items": n_items,

   677	                "per_item_fields": per_item,

   688	                "row_sha256": sha256_hex(raw),
   689	                "unblinded": exp in verdicts,
   690	                "ts": rec.get("ts"),

   297	def read_log(path):
   298	    """Read and chain-verify a results-log JSONL file.
   299	
   300	    Returns (records, raw_lines) where raw_lines are the exact byte lines
   301	    (including trailing newline). Raises ChainError on any violation.
   302	    """
   303	    try:
   304	        with open(path, "rb") as f:
   305	            data = f.read()
   306	    except FileNotFoundError:
   307	        return [], []
   308	    if not data:
   309	        return [], []
   310	    if not data.endswith(b"\n"):
   311	        raise ChainError("%s: file is not newline-terminated" % path)
   312	    raw_lines = [line + b"\n" for line in data[:-1].split(b"\n")]
   313	    records = []
   314	    prev = GENESIS
   315	    for i, raw in enumerate(raw_lines):
   316	        try:
   317	            rec = json.loads(raw.decode("utf-8"))
   318	        except (UnicodeDecodeError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
   319	            raise ChainError("%s line %d: unparseable (%s)" % (path, i, e))
   320	        if rec.get("seq") != i:
   321	            raise ChainError("%s line %d: seq=%r, expected %d" % (path, i, rec.get("seq"), i))
   322	        if rec.get("prev_sha256") != prev:
   323	            raise ChainError(
   324	                "%s line %d (seq %d): prev_sha256 mismatch — chain broken (edit or deletion upstream)"
   325	                % (path, i, i)
   326	            )
   327	        records.append(rec)
   328	        prev = sha256_hex(raw)
   329	    return records, raw_lines
   330	
   331	
   332	def log_tail_sha256(raw_lines):
   333	    return sha256_hex(raw_lines[-1]) if raw_lines else GENESIS
   334	
   335	
   336	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- JSON pointer + verdict grammar
   337	
   338	class MissingMetric(KotError):
   339	    def __init__(self, pointer):
   340	        self.pointer = pointer
   341	        super().__init__("ERR_P2_MISSING_METRIC", "pointer %r missing/null in analysis output" % pointer)
   342	
   343	
   344	_MISSING = object()

item.text: The generator’s normal CLI is mutating (it rewrites the analysis output and verdict). Because this is a frozen/read-only audit, I’m executing its actual `run()` path with only its two JSON file-write calls intercepted in memory; the log already contains an unblind marker, so no chain append is needed. This preserves the eligibility, overlay, pinned-analysis, rule, and audit-gate logic.

import importlib.util, json, pathlib, hashlib, io, contextlib
root=pathlib.Path('.').resolve()
spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('vg', root/'tools/registry/verdict-gen.py')
vg=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(vg)
captured={}
real_write=vg.kc.write_canonical_json
real_sha=vg.file_sha256
real_makedirs=vg.os.makedirs
def virtual_write(path,obj): captured[str(path)]=vg.kc.canonical_bytes(obj)
def virtual_sha(path):
 p=str(path)
 return hashlib.sha256(captured[p]).hexdigest() if p in captured else real_sha(path)
vg.kc.write_canonical_json=virtual_write
vg.file_sha256=virtual_sha
vg.os.makedirs=lambda *a,**k: None
buf=io.StringIO()

   415	        a["shuffled_recovers_geq_30"] = pt["rec"] >= RECOVERY_BAR

/bin/bash -lc "python3 tools/registry/corpus-pin.py d-adj-t; python3 - <<'PY'
import json
items=[json.loads(x) for x in open('data/d-qa-t/items/covered.jsonl')]
labels={x['id']:x for x in map(json.loads,open('data/d-adj-t/labels.jsonl'))}
resolved=[it for it in items if labels[it['id']]['gold_ext'] is not None]

## Audit outcome: PASS CONFIRMED

The `PASS-PENDING-AUDIT` verdict reproduces exactly from the current frozen record, amendments, and chained log. I found no statistical-integrity or preregistration defect.

Reproduction used the actual `verdict-gen.py` execution path with its output writes intercepted in memory. With `computed_at=2026-07-11T18:47:00Z`, the generated verdict object and analysis output were byte-for-byte identical to the on-disk files:

- Verdict: `PASS-PENDING-AUDIT`
- Fired rule: index 4, `primary_reject AND shuffled_low_recovery AND beats_gloss_self_verify`
- Analysis SHA-256: `637cc767348823ebb8d958f1b23854af817199b937b92180e072984c3e052afa`
- Eligible runs: 17; exclusions: none
- Log tail: `1ebcf7fe9a6e5f4c790d043351c06dbeadf2846541d7bd5ed664f2469cc36119`

The reproduced object matches [registry/verdicts/f2b-transfer.json](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/verdicts/f2b-transfer.json:1).

The canonical frozen-record hash independently recomputes to `b341a0901e12023d3c56bdc196be0b9c492c7d348f988416d7e9c43aade20879`, matching both the record and frozen index. The overlay’s only two changes are:

- `/pins/corpus_hashes/d-adj-t`: placeholder → `a4baa36f…`; the corpus hash independently recomputes to the same value. See [amendment 1](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/amendments/f2b-transfer/1-pin-d-adj-t-corpus.json:8).
- `/pins/harness_manifest`: placeholder → `ef3e72e6…`. See [amendment 2](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/registry/amendments/f2b-transfer/2-pin-harness-manifest.json:8).

No `/design`, endpoint, threshold, verdict-rule, hypothesis, analysis-script, or freeze field changed. The effective amended-record SHA-256 is `fcd1992508601be065ba1de7f59a8ec472e07b8eb5815fc21039aed6d509f763`.

The results log contains exactly 18 chained records: one stage-1 adjudication run, one unblind marker, and sixteen stage-2 rows. The chain, sequence numbers, `prev_sha256`, preregistration hashes, and all `config_sha256` values verify. The rows are:

- Line 1: adjudication instrument.
- Line 2: verdict-gen unblind marker.
- Lines 3–8: model-alone R1/R3 × seeds 0–2.
- Lines 9–11: kernel-verify-retry R1, k=4 × seeds.
- Lines 12–14: shuffled-kernel-verify-retry R1, k=4 × seeds.
- Lines 15–17: gloss-self-verify-retry R1, k=4 × seeds.
- Line 18: extraction instrument.

Every arm, rung, retry budget, escalation budget, and seed is a declared level in the frozen design. Every scoring arm supplies 250 external- and membership-scoring observations. See [results-log/f2b-transfer.jsonl](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/results-log/f2b-transfer.jsonl:1).

The frozen analysis script itself matches its pin, `85a8fde6…`. Its reproduced findings are:

- Primary: external-gold accuracy `0.646667 − 0.396000 = 0.250667`; one-sided 95% BCa lower bound `0.220000 > 0`, so `primary_reject=true`. The implementation is at [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:392](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:392).
- H-STRUCT: shuffled recovery `0.026596`, with one-sided 95% BCa upper bound `0.061453 < 0.30`; Holm-adjusted result is true (`p≈0.00009999`). See [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:405](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:405).
- RT-2: kernel accuracy `0.646667` versus gloss-self `0.400000`; Holm result is true (`p≈0.00009999`). Holm construction is at [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:428](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:428).

All gates are VALID:

- Adjudication: 360 adjudicated; 27/360 = 7.5% unresolved; raw agreement 324/359 = 0.9025. See [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:235](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:235).
- Engagement: decidable fraction `1.0`, attempt-0 rejection rate `0.628`, 203 final answers changed. See [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:282](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:282).
- Headroom: R1-alone `0.396 ≤ 0.85`.
- Separation: gap `0.228`, one-sided BCa lower bound `0.180 > 0`.
- Extraction instrument: 500 labelled outputs and zero failures.

The dual-scoring contrast is `lift_mem 0.256000 − lift_ext 0.250667 = 0.005333`. It is explicitly computed outside the Holm family and marked descriptive, never verdict-bearing, at [analysis/f2b_transfer.py:423](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/analysis/f2b_transfer.py:423).

The harness manifest also checks out. Re-running `--print-manifest` found 159 staged files and reproduced `ef3e72e68af1eac2eb03870d56cc5b9165785f6678bcd69b7a5ea8907692ae4b`. The remote/local staged-byte comparison fails closed at [modal_f2bt.py:185](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/f2b-transfer/modal/modal_f2bt.py:185), and the completed run records `local_manifest_matched=true` at [provenance-modal.json:5](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/f2b-transfer/results-incoming/20260711-184448-modal/provenance-modal.json:5).

Finally, I independently reconstructed the evaluation prefix: 360 generated items, 333 with non-null external labels, and exactly the first 250 resolved items in pinned rank order. All 250 are externally labelled and kernel-checkable. The fail-closed selection logic is at [f2bt_runner.py:873](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/f2b-transfer/runner/f2bt_runner.py:873).

One operational caveat: amendment 2 and the incoming GPU artifacts are currently untracked, while the log, analysis output, and verdict are modified but uncommitted. Thus this confirmation applies to the current working-tree snapshot; a clean checkout of repository `HEAD` does not yet contain the complete evidence package. This is a persistence/repository-state issue, not a defect in the reproduced statistical verdict.
